negative_cases.why_wrong: Ddt Voltage Derivative Source: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Compute a continuous-time derivative response from V(in).

Ddt Voltage Derivative Source: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use ddt() to form a voltage-domain derivative source fail.
Ddt Voltage Derivative Source: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Ddt Voltage Derivative Source: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Compute a continuous-time derivative response from V(in) fails.

Ddt Voltage Derivative Source: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use ddt() to form a voltage-domain derivative source fail.
